data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding attributable to this child; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED — FULLY covered by THIS child:
- Parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" → 33d0 AC1 (exactly one outbound applier path remains in production; retired path deleted, no dead separately-tested applier code). FULLY.
- Parent AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" → 33d0 AC1 (deletion) + 33d0 AC2 (behavioral-parity gate verified BEFORE deletion — surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did). FULLY.
- Parent SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0 AC1 + AC3 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — driven through the production path, asserting each sub-op took effect). FULLY.

CONTRIBUTED: parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via 33d0 AC4 (no capability regresses; suite green); parent SC "new tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 33d0 AC5 (combined test asserts effects/sub-op outcomes, never interaction/mock-call-counts).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT item (sub-op telemetry → story E/2359) is consistent with the roster:
- AC SSOT snapshot-entry schema + AC fetcher-validated-vs-schema + AC each-differ-reads-validated + AC round-trip contract through production path w/ real fixtures + SC producer/consumers cannot drift → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures/fake foundation) + 59ef (verified-fake shape fidelity).
- AC sub-op telemetry surfaced + AC `computed > 0 && applied == 0` fails loudly (after warn-first rollout) + SC silent no-op fails a test/canary → 2359 (AC1/AC2/AC3; depends_on 33d0 declared).
- AC bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically + AC every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded + SC bidirectional round-trips asserted / no probe encodes a bug as expected → 822a.

ANTI-FP — parent AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" is FULLY covered, NOT an absence. The parent AC verbatim names its owner (story B = sibling 8937), and 8937 owns it (description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact …" + 8937 AC5 "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note …) is committed and referenced by the new tests"). Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts), this is FULLY covered. The earlier recurring "AC6 PARTIALLY / governs new tests" finding on this ticket was raised against paraphrased wording that does not match the plan text and has been withdrawn; not re-filed.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY (OWNED by THIS child) — "collapse to single production path", "retired path deleted", SC "production path exercised, not dead path"; CONTRIBUTED — "suite green" + SC "zero change-detector"; COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — schema SSOT + fetcher/differ validation + round-trip contract (8937/fe57/59ef), sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary (2359), bidirectional round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a), documented guardrail (8937 = story B, named owner); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — "all six closed; full reconciler suite green". UNCOVERED — none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion, and THIS child fully delivers its owned share (applier collapse, retired-path deletion with parity gate, production-path exercise).
